message:
test(bubbles,bubbletea): add comprehensive bracketed paste tests

Add tests to verify bracketed paste behavior across the TUI stack:

bubbletea:
- test_parse_bracketed_paste_basic: basic paste parsing
- test_parse_bracketed_paste_empty: empty paste
- test_parse_bracketed_paste_multiline: multi-line paste
- test_parse_bracketed_paste_unicode: unicode content
- test_parse_bracketed_paste_incomplete: incomplete with more data
- test_parse_bracketed_paste_incomplete_no_more_data: incomplete at EOF
- test_parse_bracketed_paste_not_bracketed: non-paste input
- test_parse_bracketed_paste_large: large paste (10k chars)

textinput:
- test_bracketed_paste_basic: basic paste
- test_bracketed_paste_multiline_converts_newlines: newlines → spaces
- test_bracketed_paste_crlf_converts_to_space: CRLF handling
- test_bracketed_paste_respects_char_limit: char limit enforcement
- test_bracketed_paste_respects_remaining_capacity: partial capacity
- test_bracketed_paste_at_full_capacity_ignored: full capacity
- test_bracketed_paste_unfocused_ignored: unfocused state
- test_bracketed_paste_inserts_at_cursor: cursor position
- test_bracketed_paste_strips_control_chars: control char removal
- test_bracketed_paste_preserves_unicode: unicode preservation
- test_bracketed_paste_tabs_to_spaces: tab handling

textarea:
- test_bracketed_paste_basic: basic paste
- test_bracketed_paste_multiline_preserves_newlines: newlines preserved
- test_bracketed_paste_crlf_normalized: CRLF → LF
- test_bracketed_paste_respects_char_limit: char limit
- test_bracketed_paste_unfocused_ignored: unfocused state
- test_bracketed_paste_inserts_at_cursor: cursor position
- test_bracketed_paste_unicode: unicode
- test_bracketed_paste_large_content: large paste (1k chars)
- test_bracketed_paste_multiline_cursor_position: cursor after paste

Implements bd-3jg2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -177,7 +177,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3pm6","title":"demo_showcase data: action API (start job/deploy, emit logs/toasts)","description":"Create a clean *domain action API* so multiple UI surfaces can trigger the same behaviors without depending on each other.

Rationale:
- Currently Jobs page actions and Wizard completion both need to start jobs/deployments.
- If Wizard depends on Jobs page code (or vice versa), the architecture becomes tangled.

Deliverables:
- A set of pure-ish functions (or a small service struct) that:
  - create/start/cancel/retry jobs
  - start deployments / rollbacks
  - emit structured log entries
  - emit user-facing notifications (as app messages)
- The action layer should accept the current domain state + inputs and return:
  - updated state
  - side effects expressed as bubbletea `Cmd` / `AsyncCmd` (feature-gated)
  - events/messages for UI (toasts, navigation)

Implementation guidance:
- Prefer composing effects with `bubbletea::batch(...)` (toast + log + state refresh) instead of ad-hoc "do one thing" commands.
- Use `bubbletea::sequence(...)` for any multi-step workflow that must occur in order (e.g., release-terminal -> run -> restore, or staged deploy steps).

Acceptance:
- Jobs page and Wizard both call this API (no cross-dependencies between pages).
- The API is unit-testable (given state + input -> expected state/events).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.840599209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:41.700985132Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:41.700919670Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.860556382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:28.941199473Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ps4","title":"Interactions: enable mouse mode + click/scroll mapping","description":"Enable mouse support in `demo_showcase` and map mouse events to a coherent UX (scroll, click-to-focus/select).

Why:
- Mouse support is a premium signal and makes the showcase feel like a real product.
- It also validates bubbletea’s mouse plumbing (`MouseMsg` parsing + crossterm integration).

Implementation requirements:
- Program configuration:
  - run with mouse enabled (prefer `with_mouse_cell_motion()` to support hover/cell-motion where useful).
  - ensure mouse can be disabled via Settings/Config toggle.
- Scroll mapping:
  - wheel scroll should apply to the *focused pane* (viewport, list, table, file preview).
  - when nothing is explicitly focused, default to the primary content pane.
- Click mapping:
  - click selects within lists/tables/filepicker when coordinates hit rows.
  - click on dashboard cards triggers selection/drill-down.
  - click-to-focus inputs (search bars, text inputs, etc.).

Behavioral constraints:
- Mouse must not fight keyboard navigation:
  - keyboard remains first-class, mouse is additive.
  - selection/focus updates should be consistent regardless of input source.
- Headless/CI safety:
  - mouse support should be a no-op when renderer/terminal features are unavailable.

Acceptance:
- Mouse scroll feels natural in viewports/lists/tables/file preview.
- Clicking to select/focus works in the major screens.
- Disabling mouse immediately stops mouse-driven changes.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - given `MouseMsg` wheel events, verify the correct component state changes (scroll offset, selection).
  - click hit-testing logic for at least one list/table and one card grid.
- E2E (optional but preferred):
  - use the harness “raw byte” mode to inject xterm mouse sequences and assert state changes in artifacts.

Notes:
- Keep hit-testing deterministic: base calculations on known layout metrics (width/height + fixed styles).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.064610623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:49.256919092Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.093132249Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:56.981806786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:55.722943138Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Implementation idea:
- Use harmonica `Spring` to ease values toward targets.
- Drive updates via bubbletea tick/every (or async tick).
- Keep animations deterministic and bounded.

Acceptance:
- Any widget/page can request an animated value update without bespoke code.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:54.209871764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:06.871504996Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","harmonica"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:54.244746558Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-2szb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:04.402219642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:06.871476743Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:05.620584063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Acceptance:
- Any widget/page can request an animated value update without bespoke code.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:54.209871764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:22:23.647543696Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","harmonica"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:54.244746558Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-2szb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:04.402219642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:06.871476743Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:05.620584063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3rwl","title":"Jobs: implement jobs table/list + selection","description":"Implement the main Jobs/Tasks page: a dense, usable jobs table/list with selection.

Why:
- This is the core "ops console" workflow: see what’s running, what failed, and drill into details.
- It showcases Bubbles components (table/list, paginator, progress, spinner) working together.

Implementation options:
- Use `bubbles::table` for a dense table view, or
- Use `bubbles::list` for a richer list with descriptions.

Data to show (minimum):
- job name / id
- status (running/succeeded/failed/cancelled)
- progress (when applicable)
- duration / started-at
- owner/service/environment (as fits the domain model)

Interactions:
- Keyboard navigation and selection.
- Selection updates a details pane (separate task) and enables actions (start/cancel/retry).

Acceptance:
- Keyboard navigation works and feels consistent.
- Selection is stable across refreshes and filtering changes (best-effort).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:18.314504691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:58:13.418655253Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:58:13.418589240Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase","jobs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:18.330182628Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:31.091452200Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-3bz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:35.920756563Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:33.415669784Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3s4n","title":"demo_showcase theme: color profile + NO_COLOR/ASCII modes","description":"Make the demo resilient in different terminals.

Implementation notes:
- Drive behavior from `demo_showcase::Config` + lipgloss `Renderer`/ColorProfile.

Implement:
- Respect `NO_COLOR` and a CLI `--no-color` override (force ASCII).
- Respect terminal color profiles (truecolor vs 256 vs ANSI vs ASCII).
- Ensure borders/layout still look good in ASCII.

Acceptance:
- Demo remains readable and not visually broken with colors disabled.
- A11y: focus states and status colors remain distinguishable in reduced color modes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:26.492936953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:17.795629053Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","demo_showcase","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3s4n","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:41.037189952Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3s4n","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:26.508936665Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3s4n","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:36.610506249Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3s4n","depends_on_id":"bd-y145","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:37.863784681Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3spt","title":"E2E scenario: logs export/copy actions","description":"Add an end-to-end scenario that validates high-polish Logs actions (copy/export/clear).

Scenario outline:
1) Launch demo_showcase headless with deterministic seed.
2) Navigate to Logs.
3) Trigger:
   - export logs to file (and assert file written in the e2e artifact directory)
   - clear logs (and assert viewer empties)

Assertions:
- Export path is reported in structured logs.
- Exported file contains expected headings/markers.

Acceptance:
- Deterministic and CI-friendly.
- On failure, artifacts include the exported file (if any) and the last rendered Logs frame.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:51.000363578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:51.052683939Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3spt","depends_on_id":"bd-15xi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:51.052653883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3spt","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:51.041466493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3spt","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:51.036200268Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3spt","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:51.046593668Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3spt","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:51.030224731Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
